Split a table object into per-page data.frames

paginate.Rd

Single entry point that converts various supported table objects into a list of data.frames, one per page, ready to be passed to [rtf_tables()] (each data.frame carries an `rtf_blank_rows` attribute that `rtftable(read_attributes = TRUE)` consumes automatically).

Usage

paginate(x, ...)

# Default S3 method
paginate(x, ...)

# S3 method for class 'gt_tbl'
paginate(x, ...)

# S3 method for class 'list'
paginate(x, ...)

# S3 method for class 'data.frame'
paginate(
  x,
  max_rows = NULL,
  split = c("none", "rows", "group_safe", "group_force"),
  split_rows = NULL,
  group_col = NULL,
  cont_label = " (Cont.)",
  blank_rows = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

x

A supported table object (`gt_tbl`, `data.frame`/tibble, or a `list` of those).

...

Method-specific extras.

max_rows

Integer. Maximum body rows per page. Required for any `split` mode other than `"none"`.

split

Splitting strategy: `"none"` (default), `"rows"`, `"group_safe"`, or `"group_force"`. See **Splitting strategies** in the package vignette.

split_rows

Integer vector of 1-based row indices at which to start new pages. Only used when `split = "rows"`.

group_col

Column name or 1-based index identifying the group. `NULL` (default) auto-detects groups from leading-space indentation on column 1.

cont_label

Suffix appended to the group label on continuation pages (only used by group-aware splits). Default `" (Cont.)"`.

blank_rows

Blank-row specification: * `NULL` (default) — no blank rows added. * integer vector — positions within each page. * `"between_groups"` — auto-insert a blank row before every group transition within the page. * `list(...)` of any of the above — union of positions.

Value

A list of data.frames, one per page. Each carries: * `attr(., "rtf_blank_rows")` — integer positions consumed by [rtftable()] when `read_attributes = TRUE`. * `attr(., "rtf_paginate_meta")` — list with `strategy`, `page_index`, `total_pages`, `group_col`.

Details

Dispatch is by S3 class: * `paginate.gt_tbl()` — for [gt::gt()] tables. Requires the optional `gt` package; an informative error is raised otherwise. * `paginate.data.frame()` — for plain data.frames / tibbles. Where the real work happens; the gt method extracts a data.frame and delegates here. * `paginate.list()` — recurses into every element and concatenates the resulting pages in input order.

New table-object types can be supported by adding another `paginate.<class>()` method — see `vignette("paginate")` for an example.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# gt input
pages <- paginate(my_gt_tbl, max_rows = 20, split = "group_force")

doc <- rtf_document() |>
  rtf_section(page = 1, secinfo = list(header = my_hdr)) |>
  rtf_tables(pages)         # one page per data.frame

# plain data.frame input + manual split + between-group blanks
pages <- paginate(df, max_rows = 30, split = "group_safe",
                   blank_rows = "between_groups")

# list of gt tables (e.g. one per table number)
all_pages <- paginate(list(t1_gt, t2_gt), max_rows = 20,
                      split = "group_force")
} # }
